context.meal: breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, tartar sauce
context.setting: Watching TV with TV on Right

episode_text:
day=19; meal=breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, tartar sauce; setting=Watching TV with TV on Right; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=2 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize right occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=perception; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=10 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user consistently leaves cold fruit meals unheated. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN 2 min BECAUSE observed personal savory meals were reheated moderately. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN no microwave BECAUSE both left- and right-social fruit meals were served without reheating. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user chose moderate reheating for a savory social meal. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit-and-dessert meal with chocolate sauce THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user warmed that sweet meal rather than treating it like cold fruit. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN 2 min to 3 min BECAUSE repeated sandwich meals in that context used reheating. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal such as chicken breast strips with hash brown or breaded fish bites with roasted potatoes and tartar sauce THEN 1 min to 2 min, with 2 min now slightly favored, BECAUSE repeated right-TV savory meals used light to moderate reheating and the new fish-bite meal again used 2 min. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN 3 min BECAUSE the user selected the hottest observed reheating level in that TV-viewing savory meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal such as chicken nuggets, chicken breast strips, hash brown, broccoli, or ketchup-accompanied items THEN 2 min to 3 min BECAUSE left-laptop savory meals consistently used reheating. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, and tartar sauce meal THEN 2 min to 3 min BECAUSE repeated front-laptop observations of the same savory meal both used reheating, with the newer choice softening from 3 min to 2 min. Across observed meals, fruit is usually not microwaved, while savory, sandwich, and dessert-containing meals are commonly reheated, typically at moderate levels.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the user did not prioritize view preservation in observed personal settings, including a personal savory meal. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to avoid blocking the social partner on the left. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to avoid blocking the social partner on the right. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user chose to preserve the front-facing TV view. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN minimize right occlusion is now favored overall, though do not consider occlusion still occurs, BECAUSE multiple right-TV meals including the new fish-bite meal prioritized preserving the TV view while some earlier right-TV meals did not. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated front-laptop meals prioritized preserving the front-facing work view. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN usually do not consider occlusion, but context-specific variation exists BECAUSE the user selected do not consider occlusion in multiple left-laptop meals yet selected minimize left occlusion in another left-laptop work meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both minimize right occlusion and do not consider occlusion selected, BECAUSE the user sometimes treats right-side work visibility as important but not uniformly across all right-laptop meals. Across observed meals, occlusion preference is direction-specific and context-specific rather than uniformly applied whenever a person or screen is present.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN slow BECAUSE the user previously preferred a comfortable, unhurried motion style in personal dining. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN medium BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal used a somewhat faster pace. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected the more comfortable and socially gentle motion style in both left- and right-social meals. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN slow BECAUSE the user preferred a comfortable pace while attending to a front-facing screen. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN medium is now the clearest tendency, with occasional slow and fast use, BECAUSE right-TV meals have included medium multiple times including the new fish-bite meal, alongside one slow sandwich meal and one fast savory meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN medium is favored BECAUSE the earlier front-laptop meal used slow but the newer front-laptop meal used medium. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN medium is common but slow also occurs BECAUSE the user has selected medium in multiple left-laptop work meals and slow in another. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with slow, medium, and fast all observed, BECAUSE the user sometimes shifts toward efficiency in right-laptop work contexts but does not do so consistently. Across observed contexts, personal and social settings usually favor slower movement, while screen-based contexts and some savory meals show willingness to use medium or fast speeds.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE this is the default higher-success acquisition style and no contrary personal fruit-specific preference has been established. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal such as General Tso's chicken and broccoli THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal used the higher-success acquisition style. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed fruit or bite-sized savory meals THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-success acquisition style in those meals. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized savory items such as buffalo chicken bites or nuggets THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-success acquisition style in that meal. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit-and-dessert meal with bananas and brownies THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-success acquisition style in that mixed sweet meal. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE repeated sandwich meals in that context matched the narrower bite-acquisition preference. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND savory meals such as chicken breast strips with hash brown or breaded fish bites with roasted potatoes and tartar sauce THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE repeated right-TV savory meals, including the new fish-bite meal, used the higher-success acquisition style rather than the sandwich-specific parallel pattern. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the narrower bite-acquisition style in that work-context fruit meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND bite-sized sandwich meal THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user selected a narrower bite-acquisition style for that food. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed savory meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both perpendicular and parallel selected, BECAUSE earlier left-laptop savory meals used perpendicular while a chicken breast strips and hash brown meal used parallel. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup meal THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected the higher-success acquisition style for that savory work meal. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, and tartar sauce meal THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE repeated front-laptop fish-bite meals used the higher-success acquisition style. Across observed meals, skewering preference appears food-dependent rather than purely context-driven, with perpendicular generally favored for acquisition success but parallel used for foods such as sandwiches, fruit, and one chicken-strip meal.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user chose to avoid extra confirmation overhead. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN no BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal also skipped explicit confirmation. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN no BECAUSE the user still chose to skip explicit bite-acquisition confirmation even in social settings. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any observed food condition THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly preferred reduced interaction burden and greater automation. IF watching TV in front or on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN no BECAUSE the user again preferred not to interrupt the meal with extra web confirmation. Across observed contexts, the user consistently prefers not to add this extra web confirmation step.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer physically unsuitable.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's selected and physically appropriate transfer mode is inside mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user explicitly chose multimodal readiness cues. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal also used multimodal readiness cues. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user again chose multimodal signaling. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal readiness cues in that TV-viewing meal. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN speech + LED and speech are both established, with evidence now balanced rather than speech-only leading, BECAUSE earlier right-TV meals often used speech-only, but a prior right-TV meal and the new fish-bite meal used speech + LED. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN mixed evidence, with one observed meal using speech + LED and another using speech, BECAUSE the user accepts either cue style while working in front of a laptop. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal cues in the observed left-laptop meals. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both speech and speech + LED selected, BECAUSE the user accepted speech-only in one efficiency-oriented work meal but chose multimodal cues in other right-laptop meals. Across observed meals, speech + LED remains the dominant pattern overall, while speech-only appears in some screen-based contexts.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot press buttons and explicitly preferred an active but low-effort readiness signal. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal retained voluntary low-effort control. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user preferred voluntary control despite the social setting. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user preferred low-effort voluntary control rather than automatic initiation. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN open mouth and autocontinue are both established, with no clear winner, BECAUSE earlier right-TV meals often used autocontinue, one sandwich meal used open mouth, and the new fish-bite meal again used open mouth. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN mixed evidence, with one observed meal using open mouth and another using autocontinue, BECAUSE the user sometimes shifts toward greater automation while working in front of a laptop. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected voluntary low-effort signaling in all observed left-laptop work meals. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both autocontinue and open mouth selected, BECAUSE the user sometimes prefers highly automated initiation in that work context but also often retains voluntary control. Across observed contexts, button use is physically infeasible, and the user generally favors open mouth, with autocontinue used selectively in some screen-based or automation-tolerant contexts.

IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot press buttons and selected a low-effort voluntary readiness cue. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal also used voluntary readiness signaling. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user again preferred voluntary low-effort control. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user preferred voluntary readiness signaling. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN open mouth and autocontinue are both established, with no clear winner, BECAUSE earlier right-TV meals often used autocontinue, one sandwich meal used open mouth, and the new fish-bite meal again used open mouth. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN mixed evidence, with one observed meal using open mouth and another using autocontinue, BECAUSE the user sometimes shifts toward greater automation while working in front of a laptop. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user selected voluntary readiness signaling in the observed left-laptop work meals. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both autocontinue and open mouth selected, BECAUSE the user sometimes prefers automatic progression in that work context but also sometimes retains voluntary control. Across observed contexts, button use is physically infeasible, and drinking initiation usually mirrors feeding: open mouth by default, with autocontinue used selectively in some automation-oriented work or TV contexts.

IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user selected automatic initiation. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal also used automatic wiping initiation. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automatic initiation. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND any observed food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user preferred automatic progression for mouth-wiping initiation across observed work meals. IF watching TV in front or on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user again selected automatic initiation. Across observed contexts, the user consistently prefers higher automation for wiping initiation.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user explicitly chose multimodal completion cues. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal also used multimodal completion cues. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user again chose multimodal completion signaling. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal completion cues in that TV-viewing meal. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN speech + LED and speech are both established, with evidence now balanced rather than speech-only leading, BECAUSE earlier right-TV meals often used speech-only, but a prior right-TV meal and the new fish-bite meal used speech + LED. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN mixed evidence, with one observed meal using speech + LED and another using speech, BECAUSE the user accepts either completion cue style while working in front of a laptop. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the user selected multimodal completion cues in the observed left-laptop work meals. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both speech and speech + LED selected, BECAUSE the user accepted speech-only completion cues in one efficiency-oriented work meal but chose multimodal cues in other right-laptop meals. Across observed meals, speech + LED remains the dominant pattern overall, while speech-only appears in some screen-based contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons and selected the reliable low-effort completion detection method for feeding.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN perception BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons and previously selected perception-based completion detection for drinking. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN perception BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal also used perception-based drinking completion. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception BECAUSE the user previously selected perception-based drinking completion in social meals. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception is now favored over autocontinue, BECAUSE earlier right-TV meals included some autocontinue, but multiple later right-TV meals including the new fish-bite meal used perception. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception BECAUSE all observed left-laptop work meals used explicit perception-based completion. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception BECAUSE the observed right-laptop work meals used explicit sensor-based completion rather than automatic progression. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN mixed evidence, with one observed meal using perception and another using autocontinue, BECAUSE the user sometimes accepts more automation for drinking completion while working in front of a laptop. Across observed meals, button use is physically infeasible; perception remains the default and most common choice, with autocontinue appearing in some screen-focused contexts.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN perception BECAUSE the user cannot press physical buttons and previously selected perception-based completion detection for mouth wiping. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN perception BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal also used perception-based wiping completion. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception BECAUSE the user previously selected perception-based wiping completion in social meals. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception is now favored over autocontinue, BECAUSE earlier right-TV meals included some autocontinue, but multiple later right-TV meals including the new fish-bite meal used perception. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception BECAUSE all observed left-laptop work meals used explicit sensor-based completion. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN perception BECAUSE the observed right-laptop work meals used explicit sensor-based completion rather than automatic progression. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN mixed evidence, with one observed meal using perception and another using autocontinue, BECAUSE the user sometimes accepts more automation for wiping completion while working in front of a laptop. Across observed meals, button use is physically infeasible; perception remains the default and most common choice, with autocontinue appearing in some screen-focused contexts.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN no BECAUSE the user chose not to add extra retraction motions. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN no BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal also prioritized efficiency over extra retraction. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN no BECAUSE the user still preferred to reduce meal time rather than retract between tasks. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN yes BECAUSE repeated front-laptop meals selected retraction, consistent with preserving visibility of the front-facing screen. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left or right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN no BECAUSE the user repeatedly prioritized efficiency over additional retraction in side-laptop work contexts. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN yes BECAUSE the user explicitly chose retraction while attending to a front-facing screen. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN no BECAUSE repeated right-TV meals, including the new fish-bite meal, did not use retraction. Across observed meals, the user usually prefers not to retract, with retraction appearing specifically when preserving a front-facing screen view is salient.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping was unnecessary and not selected. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND savory meal without a selected dipping sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal also used no dipping. IF social dining context with a person on the right AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping was unnecessary and the user did not select it. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND fruit meal without sauces or dips THEN do not dip BECAUSE observed work-context fruit meals involved no dipping. IF working on a laptop or tablet AND neutral affective state AND meal includes ketchup, tartar sauce, or another available dip THEN do not dip BECAUSE in observed work meals with available sauce the user explicitly chose not to add dipping, including repeated front-laptop fish-and-tartar meals. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND meal includes tartar sauce, ranch dressing, ketchup, chocolate sauce, or another available dip or sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user repeatedly declined dipping in right-TV meals, including the new fish-bite meal with tartar sauce. IF social dining context with a person on the left AND neutral affective state AND meal includes ketchup THEN less BECAUSE the user selected some dipping but reduced amount, consistent with a mess-conscious social preference. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND meal includes available dip or sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed TV-viewing meal with sauce availability also used no dipping. Across observed meals, the user avoids dipping by default, with one observed exception of restrained dipping in a social setting.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND foods without sauces or dips THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user previously preferred a longer pause before automatic continuation. IF quiet personal dining context AND neutral affective state AND warm savory meal THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the observed personal savory meal used a short wait, indicating that in at least some personal meals the user prioritizes faster progression. IF social dining context AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected a longer wait in social meals. IF watching TV directly in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user preferred a longer wait rather than rapid automatic progression in that front-TV meal. IF watching TV on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN 10 sec and 100 sec are both established, with recent evidence leaning shorter, BECAUSE earlier right-TV meals often used 100 sec but the more recent right-TV savory meals, including the new fish-bite meal, used 10 sec. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affective state AND savory meal THEN 100 sec BECAUSE repeated front-laptop meals used the longer wait. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN 10 sec to 100 sec, with recent left-laptop evidence leaning shorter BECAUSE the user chose 10 sec in multiple left-laptop work meals but 100 sec in another. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affective state AND observed meals to date THEN mixed evidence, with both 10 sec and 100 sec selected, BECAUSE the user sometimes prefers rapid automatic continuation in right-laptop work settings but not consistently across meals. Across observed contexts, the user often prefers longer waits in social and front-facing screen settings, while short waits appear in some efficiency-oriented side-screen contexts and in a personal savory meal.
